title: Deliciously Garlicky Argentinian Potato Empanadas
description: Flaky pastry pockets filled with garlicky potatoes, perfect for a solo dinner, these empanadas are a traditional Argentinian dish with a twist, using a fondue pot as a cooking vessel.
instructions: Preheat the fondue pot to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, and black pepper, then gradually add melted butter and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a separate bowl, mix together diced potatoes, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Place a spoonful of the potato mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half moon shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Carefully place the empanadas into the preheated fondue pot, cooking for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the empanadas from the fondue pot and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
